title: Jaguada's moon
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{CelestialBody |image= |name=Jaguada's moon |region=*[[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Jaguada system/Legends|Jaguada system]] *[[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Trans-Hydian]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' — Based on corresponding data for [[Jaguada system/Legends|Jaguada system]] |sector=*[[Esstran sector/Legends|Esstran sector]] **[[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Worlds]] |system=[[Jaguada system/Legends|Jaguada system]]''[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader]]'' |coordinates=R-5 |xyz= |distance= |suns= |orbited=[[Jaguada/Legends|Jaguada]] |position= |moons= |routes=*[[Kamat Aegit]] *[[Kamat Krote]] |lengthday= |lengthyear= |class=Terrestrial |diameter= |atmosphere=[[Atmosphere/Legends|Type I]] {{C|breathable}} |climate= |gravity= |terrain=*Sand *Sheer cliffs |water= |interest=[[Separatist communications facility]] |flora= |fauna= |otherlife= |species= |otherspecies= |socialgroup= |language= |government= |population= |demonym= |cities= |imports= |exports= |affiliation=*[[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] *[[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] }} {{Quote|The Jaguada system, Lord Vader. More precisely, the moon of the system's only inhabited world.|First technician, to Darth Vader|}} '''Jaguada's moon''' was the sole [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Jaguada/Legends|Jaguada]], which was the only inhabited [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Jaguada system/Legends|Jaguada system]]. The moon possessed a Type I [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], which was breathable by [[Human/Legends|Humans]]. ==Description== {{Quote|How long will it take us to reach the front entrance, closest to the cliffs?''"
"''That'll dump us way down valley, Roan. A good kilometer from the drop ship.|Roan Shryne and Jula Shryne|}} Jaguada's moon was the sole [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Jaguada/Legends|Jaguada]], the only inhabited world of the [[Jaguada system/Legends|Jaguada system]], located in the [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Worlds]] region of the [[Esstran sector/Legends|Esstran sector]] in the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Trans-Hydian]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. The moon was gloomy and was frequently ravaged by the restless winds, which made travel difficult due to the blowing sand and bits of other inorganic debris. The moon had a thin Type I [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] that was breathable to [[Human/Legends|Humans]]. A part of the moon's surface had sheer cliffs made of striated rock. ==History== {{Quote|We made a couple of runs here six or so years back. Communications hardware for the hyperwave transceiver. And before you go all patriotic on me, Roan, we didn't realize that the facility would eventually be used to eavesdrop on Republic transmissions.|Jula Shryne|}} Jaguada's moon was once a part of the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]], and the ancient [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] built large geometric structures that formed a complex on the moon's surface. In [[25 BBY/Legends|25 BBY]],''[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader]]'' states that [[Jula Shryne]]'s smuggler run happened "six or so years back" before the events of the novel. ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of the ''Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader'' to [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], which would place the run in [[25 BBY/Legends|25 BBY]]. [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] turned the complex into the [[Separatist communications facility]], using equipment for the [[Hyperwave transceiver/Legends|hyperwave transceiver]] that had been hauled there by [[Jula Shryne]]. Shortly after the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]] the Confederacy abandoned the facility. Near the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] apparently left the moon alone, although they occupied Jaguada itself. A month after the war's end, [[Roan Shryne]], [[Olee Starstone]], and several other [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Jedi Purge]] survivors arrived aboard the the [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian]] [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] ''[[Drunk Dancer]]'' into the stationary orbit on the moon's far side without being detected by the Imperial [[Jaguada garrison|troops]] on Jaguada. Using a [[Dropship/Legends|drop ship]], they slipped into the moon's thin atmosphere to the landing platform of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] communications facility. Inside the facility, they used equipment to access the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] [[Memory bank/Legends|databanks]] on the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] world, [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], which inadvertently woke the early-generation [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-Series battle droids]] and [[Droideka/Legends|droidekas]] within the temple that had been shut down following the war's end. Their activities were detected from the [[Jedi Temple central security station/Legends|Temple beacon room]] by the Imperial [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Internal Security Bureau]] [[Chief/Legends|chief]], [[Armand Isard/Legends|Armand Isard]], and his crew of ISB [[Technician/Legends|technicians]], who traced the group to Jaguada's moon and relayed the information to [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. As a large [[Holomap/Legends|holomap]] displaying the Jaguada [[Star system/Legends|system]], including the planet and its moon, emerged before Vader, he stepped into the [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] and indicated Jaguada's moon in it. [[Clone Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Appo/Legends|Appo]] relayed information on the location to Vader, including the fact that an Imperial garrison was present on Jaguada. Vader then had Appo order the [[Unidentified garrison commander (Jaguada)|garrison commander]] to pursue Shryne and his cohorts. The assorted [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] and Jedi reactivated the [[Droid/Legends|droids]], reprogramming them to kill all of the stormtroopers that had arrived. ==Locations== {{Main|Separatist communications facility}} {{Quote|The moon is the site of an abandoned Separatist communications facility, whoever is in possession of the Jedi transceiver must have brought the facility's hyperspace communications network online.|Appo|}} Located in a narrow valley between two sheer cliffs of striated rock, the Separatist communications facility was once a complex of ancient, geometric structures built by the ancient Sith. The cliffs were carved with thirty-[[meter]]-tall statues, similar to statuary on the worlds of [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]] and [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], that stood silent guard over the valley with their gaze directed towards the moon's eastern horizon. Scanners classified the metal used in the construction as "unidentifiable." The complex contained [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] of parched corridors studded with gruesome statuary. One of the corridors was lined on both walls in identical recessses with identical six-meter-high statues that depicted some mythical creature from antiquity, an equal parts [[humanoid]] and winged beast. The indistinct visage was partly concealed by a hooded robe that fell to taloned feet. They were made from locally quarried stone that matched the striated rock of the cliffs that walled the complex. ==Behind the scenes== Jaguada's moon first appeared in the [[2005]] novel ''[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader]]'', written by [[James Luceno]]. The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed Jaguada and its moon in grid square R-5. ==Appearances== *''[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader]]'' {{Ab|Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book and [[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader (abridged audiobook)|audiobook]]}} *[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader (abridged audiobook)|''Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader'' abridged audiobook]] ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' {{1stID|moon of Jaguada}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Confederacy of Independent Systems moons]] [[Category:Jaguada system locations]] [[Category:Moons in the Outer Rim Territories]] [[Category:Moons in the Trans-Hydian Borderlands]] [[Category:Sith Empire moons]] [[Category:Type I atmosphere moons]]
